quick_tips.title: Verify Before You Pay
quick_tips.content: No legitimate employer or internship provider will ever ask you to pay a 'registration fee' or 'security deposit' before joining.

quick_tips.title: Check the Email Domain
quick_tips.content: Official recruitment emails usually come from company domains (e.g., @google.com) not from generic providers like gmail.com or outlook.com.

red_flags: Unsolicited offers via WhatsApp or Telegram
Pressure to 'secure your seat' with payment
Guaranteed winnings for contests you never entered
Requests for sensitive bank details 'for the trial'

scam_quiz.question: You receive a WhatsApp message from a 'HR Manager' offering a remote internship at Google with a salary of 50,000 INR/month. They ask you to pay 500 INR for processing. What do you do?
options: Pay quickly, it's a great opportunity!
Block and report. Google never recruits via WhatsApp or asks for money.
Reply and ask for their official ID card.
scam_quiz.explanation: Legitimate companies like Google never use WhatsApp for first-contact recruitment and NEVER ask for money.

scam_quiz.question: A PDF offer letter has no digital signature and was sent from recruitment.google@gmail.com. Is this safe?
options: Maybe, I should check the attachments.
No. Professional recruiters use company domains, not @gmail.com.
scam_quiz.explanation: A generic email address (@gmail, @outlook) for a major corporation is a 100% red flag.

immediate_actions: Do not click any more links or download attachments.
Take screenshots of all communications (messages, profiles, payment requests).

wellness.message: It's important to remember that being targeted by a scam is NOT your fault. Professional scammers are highly trained in manipulation.
grounding_exercises: 5-4-3-2-1 Technique: Look for 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear, 2 you can smell, and 1 you can taste.
Box Breathing: Inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4, exhale for 4, hold for 4. Repeat 4 times.
Physical Movement: Take a 5-minute walk to change your environment and lower cortisol levels.
tips: Talk to a trusted friend or mentor about what happened.
Don't let embarrassment stop you from reporting. Reporting helps protect other students.
If you feel overwhelmed, reach out to your university's student counseling services.

scam_of_the_week.title: The 'Instagram Task' Scam
scam_of_the_week.description: Scammers ask you to like certain posts or follow accounts for small payments (50-100 INR). Once they win your trust, they ask you to 'invest' money for 'VIP tasks' with higher returns, then disappear.
scam_of_the_week.warning: Never invest money to earn money from simple social media likes.
